body_txt: It's raining , and a mother (sometimes a mother and father) is carrying a baby in a basket. The baby is wrapped up in a blanket, but is obviously a main character. The parent(s) (who may or may not be kept anonymous by a cloak (or cloaks)), for one reason or another , cannot afford to raise the baby themself (or themselves). So the mother goes up to a doorstep, puts the baby down, may or may not add a letter and/or an Orphan's Plot Trinket , rings the doorbell or knocks on the door and slips quietly into the night. The door is opened by the staff of the orphanage, or the child's soon-to-be adoptive parents if a private home was chosen instead, and it just so happens that someone is always home and awake to answer the door, thus sparing the baby from dying of exposure. Because of the Law of Conservation of Detail , if the baby was left at the home of someone who contacts the authorities and is responsible but doesn't adopt them this scene is generally skipped. Cut to several years later ... In a (less sympathetic) variation of this trope, overlapping with Parental Abandonment , one biological parent abandons a child on the doorstep of the other parent, dumping all the responsibility for raising it on them. Typically it's the mother leaving the child to the father; due to Double Standard expectations this is seen as much worse than a father walking out and becoming a Disappeared Dad , and will likely be the source of a great deal of resentment if the relevant parties ever reunite. This title is a pun on Doorstep Baby, and is not to be confused using a baby as a doorstop. For that, you're looking for Hilariously Abusive Childhood or Human Doorstop . Also not to be confused with Doorstopper as in a very long book, for that matter. Also known as a "foundling". Often causes Changeling Fantasy . See also Parental Abandonment , Missing Mom . Lead-in to Moses in the Bulrushes and Muggle Foster Parents . The pet version is Abandoned Pet in a Box . A dwarf criminal may exploit this trope by pulling a Goo Goo Getup . May or may not overlap with Porch Pirate . Sometimes results in Parental Denial .
